items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits for federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens (effective Oct. 1, 2026)
items.summary: CMS released a State Health Official letter implementing section 71109 of the Working Families Tax Cut legislation (Public Law 119-21), limiting when states can claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals in certain noncitizen categories. The guidance says the change begins October 1, 2026 and notes exceptions such as continued federal matching for emergency medical conditions and that it does not affect the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant women in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: IRS reminder: request a federal tax filing extension by April 15, 2026 to file by Oct. 15, 2026
items.summary: The IRS said taxpayers can get an extension to file their 2025 federal return until October 15, 2026, but must request it by April 15, 2026. The IRS emphasized an extension is only for filing (not paying) and that taxpayers should estimate and pay any tax owed by April 15 to avoid penalties and interest.

items.title: HUD rescinds PIH 2024-17 streamlined waiver process for homelessness admissions in HCV/Public Housing
items.summary: HUD issued Notice PIH 2026-04 rescinding Notice PIH 2024-17, which provided a streamlined process for regulatory waivers intended to expedite admission for people experiencing homelessness into Housing Choice Voucher (HCV), Project-Based Voucher (PBV), and Public Housing programs. HUD said waivers already approved or granted under the prior process remain valid until they expire under their approval terms.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: HUD Notice PIH 2026-01 makes $10M available for additional HUD-VASH administrative fees; applications due March 2, 2026
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-01 announced up to $10 million in additional administrative fee funding for public housing agencies administering HUD-VASH vouchers to support leasing and program administration. The notice says applications must be received by midnight on March 2, 2026 (in the PHA’s time zone) and funds must be used exclusively for eligible HUD-VASH administrative expenses.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: USDA approves six state SNAP waivers to change what items count as eligible ‘food for purchase’ starting in 2026
items.summary: USDA announced it approved SNAP waivers for Hawai‘i, Missouri, North Dakota, South Carolina, Virginia, and Tennessee that amend the statutory definition of eligible food purchases to exclude certain items each state proposed. USDA said the waivers are intended to limit purchases of highly processed/junk foods and will go into effect beginning in 2026, with implementation timelines varying by state.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit amounts and reiterates April 2026 food package compliance requirement
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum 2026-2 set FY 2026 monthly cash-value benefit (CVV/B) amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo also reiterated that recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: Workforce Pell proposed rule: public comment window through April 8, 2026 (Pell usable for short workforce programs starting July 2026)
items.summary: The Education Department proposed rules to implement Workforce Pell, a change that would allow eligible students to use Pell Grants for certain workforce programs starting in July 2026, including programs as short as 8 weeks. The proposed rule’s public comment period was open through April 8, 2026.
items.source: National Association of Workforce Development Professionals
